extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu executed a solo lead-out through technical finale for PHILIPSEN Jasper, who won after 20-rider split formed in crosswind panic 10km out despite winds not materializing.
tactical_patterns: YATES VAN DER POEL's willingness to lead-out solo through technical 2km finale reveals team confidence sprinter will convert positioning.
GANNA Filippo's bridging effort brought PHILIPSEN across to front 20-rider split, demonstrating leaders' domestiques create sprint advantages beyond leadout.
Crosswind panic at 10km fractured peloton despite winds not materializing, proving nervous positioning more decisive than actual weather conditions.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: converted perfectly from VAN DER POEL leadout, took cyclamen jersey from JACOBSEN after yesterday's second place
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: demonstrated classics-level ability threading technical finale out-of-saddle for full 2km before releasing sprinter at 175m
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: fast-finished to second without dedicated leadout, showing independent sprint ability when positioned correctly
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: podium finish signals upward trajectory, positioning well without full leadout train support
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: yesterday's winner completely out-of-sorts, used barriers shoulder to make room suggesting positioning collapse
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: bridging work for ROGLIČ protection brought PHILIPSEN to front group, revealing dual-purpose domestique value
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: safety-first positioning at front anticipating crosswinds, risk-averse GC tactics before mountain stage
